summary.predicted_base_color: Phaeomelanin — cream / yellow / red (e/e overrides K, A, B loci for coat; liver/brown nose and pads from B/D loci)
summary.predicted_pattern: Solid phaeomelanin coat — no eumelanin in hair regardless of K or A locus. Skin pigment (liver/brown nose and pads) is determined by B and D loci independently.
summary.predicted_dilution: Not applicable to coat (phaeomelanin unaffected by D locus)
summary.predicted_white: Not detectable from SNP data (S locus limited; W requires structural variant)
summary.predicted_merle: Not detectable from SNP imputation — requires PCR or long-read sequencing
summary.caveat: E, K, B, D loci called from Dog10K GLIMPSE2 imputed BCF (causal SNPs). A locus sable (ay/aw) requires structural variant analysis not available here. Merle (M) and extreme white (W) require PCR or long-read. Commercial tests (Embark, Wisdom Panel) cover additional alleles.
summary.irf4_note: No IRF4 deletion detected in this sample.

E.name: Extension locus
E.role: Master pigment switch: eumelanin (black/brown) vs phaeomelanin (yellow/red)
E.phenotype_contribution: e/e → all coat pigment is yellow/red regardless of other loci
E.interpretation: Homozygous e/e from proxy SNP only (chr5:64186854, CPNE7 intron, alt allele freq ≈29%). The actual MC1R p.Arg306* coding position (chr5:63922309) is not in the Dog10K panel; BAM pileup shows no reads — insufficient coverage to confirm or exclude the causal allele. If this dog shows eumelanin pigmentation (black/brown coat), the proxy call is likely a false positive and the true genotype is E/e or E/E. If the dog is cream/yellow, e/e remains plausible. If the proxy call is a false positive, the coat would be chocolate based on K (KB/KB) and B (b/b) loci. Phenotype confirmation is required to distinguish e/e (cream/yellow) from chocolate.

observed_variants.effect: e haplotype proxy SNP (CPNE7 intron, chr5:64186854) — in LD with e allele in Dog10K panel but NOT the causal MC1R p.Arg306* coding variant

K.name: Dominant black locus
K.role: KB locks melanocytes in eumelanin production, overriding the A locus
K.phenotype_contribution: KB/- → solid eumelanin; ky/ky → A locus controls patterning

alleles_reference.KB: Dominant black — one copy = solid black

A.name: Agouti locus
A.role: Controls eumelanin/phaeomelanin switching within the hair shaft
A.phenotype_contribution: Only expressed when ky/ky at K locus; determines sable/tan-points/solid pattern
A.interpretation: ASIP coding positions not found; ay/aw require structural variant analysis

B.name: Brown locus
B.role: Modifies eumelanin color: B → black, b/b → brown/liver/chocolate
B.phenotype_contribution: b/b converts all black pigment to brown; no effect on phaeomelanin

D.name: Dilution locus
D.role: Melanosome transport: d/d dilutes pigment (black → blue, brown → isabella)
D.phenotype_contribution: d/d lightens all eumelanin; phaeomelanin unaffected
D.interpretation: No dilute alleles detected (D/D) — full pigment

M.name: Merle locus
M.role: SINE insertion causes mosaic pigment dilution producing merle pattern
M.phenotype_contribution: Not detectable from SNP imputation — requires PCR or long-read

alleles_reference.M: Merle (dominant, PMEL SINE insertion — not detectable from SNP data)

M.interpretation: Merle (PMEL SINE insertion) not detectable from SNP imputation — PCR required

S.name: Spotting locus
S.role: Controls melanocyte migration extent → white spotting area
S.phenotype_contribution: sp/sp → piebald; limited resolution from single SNP
S.interpretation: MITF spotting variant not found in Dog10K panel

W.name: White locus
W.role: Extreme white spotting; dominant W linked to deafness risk
W.phenotype_contribution: Not detectable from short-read SNP data

alleles_reference.W: Extreme white (dominant, KIT structural — not detectable from SNP data)

W.interpretation: KIT extreme white (structural variant) not detectable from SNP data

method: Coat color genotyping from GLIMPSE2 Dog10K imputed BCF (min GP=0.8). Causal SNPs queried at known canFam4 positions; BAM pileup fallback for sites not in panel. Compound heterozygosity handled for B (b1/b2) and D (d1/d2) loci.
